table-386-386.caption: Write down the ruling of Beacon of Immortality.
data: If the target player's life total is negative, doubling it will result in a larger negative number. For example, -5 becomes -10.
If a player's life total increases as a result of Beacon of Immortality, abilities that trigger "whenever you gain life" will trigger.
You can target any player, including yourself or an opponent. The effect applies to the targeted player's current life total.
The "shuffle Beacon of Immortality into its owner's library" effect happens as the last part of the spell's resolution.
Doubling a player's life total does not affect the amount of commander damage they have taken.
If multiple copies of Beacon of Immortality resolve targeting the same player, each will double that player's current life total independently.
